reasoning: As of mid-October 2025, SpaceX has conducted five Starship launches in the calendar year 2025 (Flights 7 through 11). To reach the target range of 10 to 12 successful launches, SpaceX would need to conduct at least five more launches in the remaining 11 weeks of the year (Flights 12 through 16). This would require an unprecedented cadence of approximately one launch every 15-18 days, which is a significant acceleration from the current 2025 average of one launch every two months. Furthermore, reports indicate that the aggressive '25 launches per year' goal has been shifted to 2026. Therefore, the probability of reaching the specific 10-12 range in 2025 is extremely low. Even if they achieved a record pace, they would more likely end the year with 6 or 7 launches total.
